User.title: User
User.description: Represents a registered user of the Neuro-Fast platform, holding application-specific profile data and role assignments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for contact and identification within the application.

roleIds.description: References to Roles assigned to the user, defining permissions and responsibilities. (Relationship: User N:N Role)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Defines a user's permissions and responsibilities within the Neuro-Fast system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

name.description: The name of the role (e.g., 'DarkStore Manager', 'Rider', 'Admin').

description.description: A detailed description of the role's purpose and responsibilities.

permissions.description: A list of granular permissions associated with this role (e.g., 'view_inventory', 'manage_forecasts').

DarkStore.title: DarkStore
DarkStore.description: Represents a quick-commerce dark store location, a central hub for inventory and deliveries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DarkStore entity.

name.description: The name of the dark store (e.g., 'Neuro-Fast London North').

address.description: The street address of the dark store.

city.description: The city where the dark store is located.

state.description: The state or province where the dark store is located.

zipCode.description: The postal or ZIP code of the dark store.

country.description: The country where the dark store is located.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the dark store.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the dark store.

managerUserId.description: Reference to the User who is the primary manager of this dark store. (Relationship: User 1:N DarkStore)

status.description: The current operational status of the dark store (e.g., 'Operational', 'Planned', 'Closed').

openingHours.description: A textual representation of the dark store's operating hours (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 8am-8pm').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the dark store record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the dark store record was last updated.

Rider.title: Rider
Rider.description: Represents a delivery rider associated with one or more dark stores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rider entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User profile for the rider. (Relationship: User 1:1 Rider)

darkStoreIds.description: References to DarkStores the rider is associated with for deliveries. (Relationship: Rider N:N DarkStore)

currentStatus.description: The rider's current operational status (e.g., 'Available', 'On Delivery', 'Offline').

vehicleType.description: The type of vehicle used by the rider (e.g., 'Bicycle', 'Motorbike', 'Car').

licenseNumber.description: The rider's driver's license or equivalent identification number.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rider record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the rider record was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Defines a generic product (SKU) that can be stocked across multiple dark stores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit, a globally unique identifier for the product.

name.description: The common name of the product.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the product.

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

unitOfMeasure.description: The standard unit of measure for this product (e.g., 'pcs', 'kg', 'liter').

vendorId.description: Reference to the primary Vendor supplying this product. (Relationship: Vendor 1:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents the specific stock level and details of a Product at a particular DarkStore.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product definition. (Relationship: Product 1:N InventoryItem)

darkStoreId.description: Reference to the DarkStore where this inventory is held. (Relationship: DarkStore 1:N InventoryItem)

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the product available in stock at the dark store.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum desired stock level for this item, triggering alerts if below.

maxStockLevel.description: The maximum desired stock level for this item, used for optimizing order quantities.

costPrice.description: The cost price of a single unit of this product.

sellingPrice.description: The selling price of a single unit of this product.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last update to the stock level or inventory details.

SalesRecord.title: SalesRecord
SalesRecord.description: Records individual sales transactions, crucial for calculating real-time sales velocity and demand.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SalesRecord entity.

darkStoreId.description: Reference to the DarkStore where the sale occurred. (Relationship: DarkStore 1:N SalesRecord)

productId.description: Reference to the Product sold. (Relationship: Product 1:N SalesRecord)

quantitySold.description: The quantity of the product sold in this transaction.

salePrice.description: The price at which a single unit of the product was sold.

transactionId.description: A unique identifier for the broader transaction or order this sale belongs to, if applicable.

Forecast.title: Forecast
Forecast.description: Stores predictive demand forecasts generated by the AI for specific inventory items over defined periods.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Forecast entity.

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem for which this forecast is made. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N Forecast)

forecastPeriodStart.description: The start timestamp of the period for which the forecast is valid.

forecastPeriodEnd.description: The end timestamp of the period for which the forecast is valid.

predictedDemand.description: The predicted quantity of demand for the inventory item during the forecast period.

confidenceScore.description: A score indicating the AI model's confidence in the accuracy of this prediction (e.g., 0 to 1).

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this forecast was generated by the AI model.

modelVersion.description: Identifier or version of the AI model used to generate this forecast.

Vendor.title: Vendor
Vendor.description: Represents a supplier of products to the dark stores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vendor entity.

name.description: The name of the vendor or supplier.

contactPerson.description: The primary contact person at the vendor.

email.description: The contact email address for the vendor.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the vendor.

address.description: The physical address of the vendor.

apiEndpoint.description: Optional API endpoint for automated interaction with the vendor's systems.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the vendor record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vendor record was last updated.

ProcurementOrder.title: ProcurementOrder
ProcurementOrder.description: Represents an order placed or suggested for restocking inventory at a dark store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProcurementOrder entity.

darkStoreId.description: Reference to the DarkStore initiating the procurement order. (Relationship: DarkStore 1:N ProcurementOrder)

vendorId.description: Reference to the Vendor from whom items are ordered. (Relationship: Vendor 1:N ProcurementOrder)

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who generated or approved the procurement order. Can also indicate system/AI generation if applicable. (Relationship: User 1:N ProcurementOrder)

status.description: The current status of the procurement order (e.g., 'Suggested', 'Pending Approval', 'Approved', 'Ordered', 'Received', 'Cancelled').

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the procurement order was created or suggested.

expectedDeliveryDate.description: The anticipated date and time of delivery for the ordered items.

totalCost.description: The calculated total cost of all items in the procurement order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the procurement order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the procurement order record was last updated.

ProcurementOrderItem.title: ProcurementOrderItem
ProcurementOrderItem.description: Represents an individual line item within a ProcurementOrder.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProcurementOrderItem entity.

procurementOrderId.description: Reference to the Procurement Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: ProcurementOrder 1:N ProcurementOrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProcurementOrderItem)

orderedQuantity.description: The quantity of the product requested in this order item.

unitPriceAtOrder.description: The price per unit of the product at the time the order was placed.

receivedQuantity.description: The actual quantity of the product received for this order item.

status.description: The status of this specific order item (e.g., 'Ordered', 'Received', 'Shortage', 'Backordered').

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Represents a system-generated notification or warning based on inventory, forecasts, or operational conditions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

darkStoreId.description: Reference to the DarkStore to which this alert pertains. (Relationship: DarkStore 1:N Alert)

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem the alert is about, if applicable. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N Alert)

type.description: The category or type of the alert (e.g., 'Low Stock', 'High Demand', 'Delivery Delay').

message.description: A detailed message describing the alert.

severity.description: The severity level of the alert (e.g., 'Critical', 'Warning', 'Info').

status.description: The current status of the alert (e.g., 'New', 'Acknowledged', 'Resolved').

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the alert was generated by the system.

resolvedByUserId.description: Optional reference to the User who acknowledged or resolved this alert. (Relationship: User 1:N Alert)

resolvedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the alert was resolved.

definition.description: Stores core user profiles. Each user can access their own document. Admins may have broader access via dedicated roles. This document is private to the user; any collaborative data is handled separately or through denormalization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Defines system-wide roles and their associated permissions. These are static definitions, primarily read-only for most users, and managed by application administrators. Not used directly for authorization checks, but for role content.

params.name: roleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the role (e.g., 'darkstore_manager', 'rider').

definition.description: A dedicated collection for application administrators. Presence of a document with the user's UID in this collection grants admin privileges, enabling efficient DBAC checks with `exists()`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'admin' role.

definition.description: Represents individual dark store locations. Each document includes `managerUserId` and a denormalized `members` map (`{uid: 'role'}`) for explicit authorization across the dark store and its subcollections, ensuring Authorization Independence for all related data.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific dark store.

definition.description: Stores profiles for delivery riders. The `riderId` matches the associated `User.id` for self-ownership. Includes `userId` and a `darkStoreMemberships` map (`{darkStoreId: {role: 'associated_rider', managerId: 'darkStoreManagerUid'}}`) denormalizing manager UIDs from associated DarkStores, enabling secure list/read for managers (QAP) without `get()` calls.

params.name: riderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the rider, which is typically the associated user's UID.

definition.description: A global catalog of products. These documents are generally read-only for most users and managed by administrators or system processes.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: A global list of product vendors/suppliers. These documents are generally read-only for most users and managed by administrators or procurement managers.

params.name: vendorId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific vendor.

definition.description: Represents specific stock levels of a product at a given dark store. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the parent `DarkStore` for authorization independence, enabling direct rule evaluation.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent dark store.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific inventory item within the dark store.

definition.description: Records individual sales transactions at a dark store. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the parent `DarkStore` for authorization independence.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent dark store.

params.name: salesRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific sales record.

definition.description: Stores predictive demand forecasts for specific inventory items. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the grandparent `DarkStore` for authorization independence.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent dark store.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent inventory item.

params.name: forecastId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific forecast.

definition.description: Represents an order placed or suggested for restocking inventory. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the parent `DarkStore` for authorization independence.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent dark store.

params.name: procurementOrderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific procurement order.

definition.description: Represents an individual line item within a procurement order. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the grandparent `DarkStore` for authorization independence.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent dark store.

params.name: procurementOrderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent procurement order.

params.name: procurementOrderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific procurement order item.

definition.description: Stores system-generated notifications or warnings. Includes denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map from the parent `DarkStore` for authorization independence.

params.name: darkStoreId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent dark store.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific alert.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Neuro-Fast prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Agnostic Rules (QAPs) by systematically applying denormalization and structural segregation. The core principle is that security rules should be simple, explicit, and avoid cross-document `get()` operations, especially across collection boundaries.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **Top-Level Entities (User, DarkStore, Rider, Product, Vendor, App Roles):**
    *   `/users/{userId}` documents are privately owned by the `request.auth.uid` corresponding to `userId`. Admin users (identified by presence in `/app_admins/{userId}`) have broader access.
    *   `/darkStores/{darkStoreId}` documents are the central hub for operations. To ensure Authorization Independence for itself and its extensive subcollections, each `DarkStore` document will include a `members` map (e.g., `members: { uid1: 'manager', uid2: 'rider_admin', ... }`). This map explicitly defines roles for all users interacting with *this specific dark store*.
    *   `/riders/{riderId}` documents (where `riderId` is the `User.id` for the rider) are self-owned (`userId == request.auth.uid`). To allow `DarkStore` managers to list and manage riders without `get()` calls, each `Rider` document will also contain a `darkStoreMemberships` map (e.g., `darkStoreMemberships: { darkStoreId1: { role: 'associated_rider', managerId: 'darkStoreManagerUid1' }, ...}`). This denormalizes the `managerId` of each associated `DarkStore` directly into the `Rider` document. This allows rules to check `request.auth.uid` against the `managerId` within this map, making `list` operations secure and performant.

2.  **Hierarchical Entities (DarkStore Subcollections):**
    *   All subcollections under `/darkStores/{darkStoreId}/...` (e.g., `inventoryItems`, `salesRecords`, `procurementOrders`, `alerts`) will inherit and explicitly denormalize the `members` map from their parent `DarkStore` document into each of their own documents (e.g., `InventoryItem.darkStoreMembers`, `Alert.darkStoreMembers`).
    *   This ensures that any security rule for documents within these subcollections can directly evaluate `resource.data.darkStoreMembers[request.auth.uid]` without needing to perform a `get()` call to the parent `DarkStore` document. This is critical for maintaining transactional integrity and simplifying debugging.
    *   For `ProcurementOrderItem`, it will inherit the `darkStoreMembers` from its grandparent `DarkStore`.

**QAPs (Query-Agnostic Rules) Support:**

*   **Structural Segregation:** Collections like `/products` and `/vendors` are global read-only for most users, simplifying their security posture. `/app_admins` uses existence checks for quick role verification.
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** `/users/{userId}` ensures `request.auth.uid` matches `userId` for self-access, which is inherently QAP-compliant.
*   **Denormalized Membership for `DarkStore` and Subcollections:** The `members` map in `DarkStore` documents and the denormalized `darkStoreMembers` map in subcollection documents allow `list` queries to be secured. A client application would typically query for documents within a specific `darkStoreId` (e.g., `/darkStores/london_north/inventoryItems`). The security rule can then directly check the `darkStoreMembers` field of the `InventoryItem` document for `request.auth.uid`'s role, ensuring that only authorized users can list the data without needing to filter on the server side (which is not how rules work).
*   **Denormalized `darkStoreMemberships` for `Riders`:** For a manager to list riders, they can query `/riders` with a `where` clause on `associatedDarkStoreIds` containing one of their managed `DarkStore` IDs. The rule can then check if `request.auth.uid` is present in the `darkStoreMemberships` map as a manager for any of the rider's associated dark stores. This ensures that the client's query, combined with the denormalized data, allows secure and efficient `list` operations without violating QAP principles.
